incidents.victim: Organizations in India and Russia (industrial, consulting, retail, transportation)
incidents.vector: Tax-themed phishing emails impersonating Indian and Russian tax authorities, delivering a Rust-based loader that drops the previously undocumented ABCDoor Python backdoor and a customized ValleyRAT.
incidents.impact: Kaspersky logged more than 1,600 malicious messages between January and February. ABCDoor enables remote screen streaming, keyboard/mouse control, clipboard theft and file operations on compromised hosts. Campaign marks the group's first known expansion into Russian targets.

incidents.vector: Active exploitation of CVE-2026-41940, a critical (CVSS 9.8) authentication bypass in cPanel & WHM that grants full control over a host system, its databases and managed websites.
incidents.impact: CISA issued an emergency directive requiring federal agencies to patch by May 3. Major hosting providers including Hosting.com, Namecheap, KnownHost, HostPapa and InMotion firewalled customers off the product. Evidence of in-the-wild exploitation since February; thousands of cPanel instances reportedly exposed.

incidents.vector: Unspecified intrusion into Moldova's national healthcare database; no ransom demand was made.
incidents.impact: Personal and financial data on virtually every adult Moldovan citizen and foreigner enrolled in national health insurance was stolen; CNAM said up to 30% of records were impacted (stolen or restored from backup). Theft comes amid heightened Russian threats against Moldova.

incidents.victim: Retail, hospitality, healthcare, technology, transportation and logistics organizations
incidents.vector: Voice phishing (vishing) impersonating IT support to direct employees to SSO-themed adversary-in-the-middle (AiTM) pages, capturing credentials and live session tokens to pivot into SSO-integrated SaaS applications and register attacker-controlled MFA devices.
incidents.impact: Active and ongoing campaign tracked variously as CORDIAL SPIDER, SNARKY SPIDER, BlackFile, UNC6671 and CL-CRI-1116. Operators have demanded seven-figure ransoms, swatted executives to pressure payment, and exfiltrated data from Salesforce, SharePoint, Microsoft Graph and internal repositories.

incidents.vector: Mirai-variant botnet built on TP-Link Archer AX21 routers vulnerable to CVE-2023-1389, plus DNS reflection abuse, operated through compromised infrastructure of Brazilian DDoS-mitigation firm Huge Networks following a January 2026 intrusion that exposed the CEO's SSH keys.
incidents.impact: Sustained DDoS campaign against small Brazilian ISPs over months. Exposed archive contained Python attack scripts, Huge Networks IPs used to launch attacks, and command history showing target prefixes attacked for 10–60 seconds with four parallel processes per host.

incidents.vector: Stolen by ShinyHunters as part of a 2026 multi-victim campaign also affecting Zara, 7-Eleven, Udemy and Medtronic; intrusion details not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Personal data on 7.5 million Carnival customers leaked online by ShinyHunters.

incidents.vector: Email-bombing followed by Microsoft Teams messages from external accounts impersonating IT help desk; victims directed to a fake 'Mailbox Repair Utility' that installs the SnowBelt malicious browser extension, then SnowGlaze and SnowBasin.
incidents.impact: Mandiant attributes the campaign to UNC6692. SnowBelt acts as a browser-based backdoor enabling persistent corporate-account access, lateral movement and downloading of follow-on tooling. Credential-harvesting page deliberately rejects first two passwords to capture them twice.

incidents.victim: U.S. federal civilian agencies and global government, financial, defense and military networks
incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2025-20333 and CVE-2025-20362 in Cisco ASA/Firepower devices to deploy the FIRESTARTER backdoor, which manipulates the Cisco Service Platform mount list to survive standard reboots and firmware patches; only a hard power-cycle removes it.
incidents.impact: CISA and the UK NCSC jointly published a malware analysis report and CISA updated Emergency Directive 25-03. CISA detected FIRESTARTER on a federal agency Cisco device months after patches were applied. Unit 42 observed exploitation of 12 federal IPs, 11 state/local government IPs, and government IPs in 14 other countries; activity tied to Storm-1849 and the ArcaneDoor campaign.

incidents.victim: U.S. consumers (defrauded by Southeast Asian scam compounds)
incidents.vector: Pig-butchering cryptocurrency investment fraud run from forced-labor compounds in Burma and Cambodia, with victim funds laundered through cryptocurrency networks.
incidents.impact: DOJ Scam Center Strike Force charged two Chinese nationals managing a Burma compound, seized a Telegram recruitment channel and 503 fraudulent investment websites, and restrained over $700M in cryptocurrency. Treasury (OFAC) sanctioned Cambodian Senator Kok An and 28 individuals/entities. IC3-reported losses to investment scams hit $7.2B in 2025; estimated $64B globally.

incidents.vector: Phishing attack compromising Klöckner's Signal account, attributed by German authorities to Russian state hackers as part of a broader Signal/WhatsApp targeting campaign.
incidents.impact: Russian operators reportedly spied on a sensitive Signal group that included Chancellor Friedrich Merz; Merz's own account was not compromised. Triggered Bundestag-wide guidance to switch from Signal to Wire.

incidents.vector: Diversion of an outbound foreign-debt repayment originally destined for Australia; intrusion vector not disclosed.
incidents.impact: $2.5 million stolen from the ministry; Sri Lanka has asked Australian law enforcement to assist the investigation.

incidents.vector: Unauthorized access to UK Biobank participant data; full vector not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Personal information on 500,000 medical-research volunteers offered for sale on the Alibaba marketplace.

incidents.vector: Compromise of a Microsoft Exchange email server at the embassy; same group has hit more than 5,000 Exchange servers in 2026.
incidents.impact: Chinese hackers stole inboxes of Cuban diplomats to gather intelligence on the U.S. naval blockade of Iran.

incidents.victim: Checkmarx KICS users (Bitwarden CLI confirmed downstream victim)
incidents.vector: Second compromise within a month of the KICS vulnerability scanner: malicious versions of the KICS Docker image, GitHub Action and VS Code extensions were published.
incidents.impact: Bitwarden CLI is the first major confirmed downstream victim of the supply-chain incident; Checkmarx confirmed the second compromise on April 22.

incidents.vector: AI hobbyists used a contractor's leaked credentials and guessed Mythos operating endpoints to log in on launch day.
incidents.impact: Anthropic said the intruders did not run malicious prompts and appeared only to 'play around,' but the unauthorized access to a flagship offensive-cyber AI platform underscores supply-chain risk for AI security tools.

incidents.vector: Phishing emails delivering a credential-harvesting page targeting Microsoft Exchange and Outlook Web Access accounts; the malicious page was generated using the Softr AI-powered web-application platform — first documented use of an AI tool in a Talos IR phishing campaign.
incidents.impact: Talos IR Q1 2026 trends report flagged phishing as the top initial-access vector (over a third of engagements) and highlighted AI-generated phishing infrastructure as lowering the barrier to entry for less-skilled actors.

incidents.victim: Twilio, LastPass, DoorDash, Mailchimp, Marks & Spencer (historical victims)
incidents.vector: Tens of thousands of SMS-phishing messages led to corporate intrusions; data was reused for SIM-swap attacks against cryptocurrency investors.
incidents.impact: Senior Scattered Spider member Tyler Robert Buchanan ('Tylerb'), 24, of Scotland, pleaded guilty in U.S. federal court to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. DOJ said he stole at least $8M in virtual currency. Sentencing August 21; statutory max 22 years.

incidents.victim: Five U.S. companies (nonprofit, hospitality, financial services, retail, medical) and additional U.S. victims
incidents.vector: Three U.S. cybersecurity-industry insiders — including DigitalMint ransomware negotiators Angelo Martino and Kevin Martin, and Sygnia incident-response manager Ryan Goldberg — deployed BlackCat/AlphV ransomware against victims, with Martino additionally exploiting his ransomware-negotiator role to leak insurer limits and negotiating positions to attackers.
incidents.impact: Goldberg and Martin sentenced April 30 to four years each; Martino pleaded guilty April 21, sentencing July 9. Combined extorted $75.3M in ransoms (including $26.8M from a nonprofit and $25.7M from a financial-services firm). $10M in assets, two Florida properties and a luxury fishing boat seized.

incidents.attacker: Criminal gang — data extortion (claimed by ShinyHunters)
incidents.vector: Lumma Stealer infection at AI vendor Context.ai (originating from a Roblox-cheats search) yielded an OAuth token to a Vercel employee's Google Workspace account; attacker pivoted to Vercel internal systems and enumerated environment variables across customer accounts.
incidents.impact: A 'small number' of Vercel customers were impacted; nearly a petabyte of Vercel logs analyzed. ShinyHunters claim the attack and are selling alleged access keys, source code and databases. Mandiant and CrowdStrike are running incident response. Downstream customers including OpenAI's Mac apps required updates.

incidents.vector: Sustained, sophisticated distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attack that intensified throughout April 16 and continued in repeat waves through the week.
incidents.impact: Days-long intermittent outage of the Bluesky platform beginning April 15; Mastodon's flagship instance was hit by a similar DDoS attack two days later.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of a vulnerability in the Kelp DAO platform allowing attackers to withdraw funds as rsETH (restaked Ether) tokens.
incidents.impact: $292 million in crypto assets stolen — the largest cryptocurrency heist of 2026 to date.

incidents.victim: French Agence Nationale des Titres Sécurisés (ANTS)
incidents.vector: Unauthorized access to the ANTS portal, France's identity-document agency that processes passport, national ID, residence-permit and driver's-license applications. Vector not disclosed.
incidents.impact: Personal data — including login credentials, names, emails, dates of birth, account identifiers and possibly postal addresses, places of birth and phone numbers — may have been disclosed for individual and professional accounts. Detected April 15; investigation ongoing.

incidents.victim: Israeli water-management and desalination operators (intended target)
incidents.vector: ZionSiphon OT-targeting malware designed to identify Israeli water-utility software, then push pump-pressure and chlorine settings to maximum. Geofencing logic failed due to an XOR encoding bug, causing the malware to self-delete on infection.
incidents.impact: Darktrace identified the malware on VirusTotal; politically motivated strings reference 'support of brothers in Iran, Palestine, and Yemen' and 'poisoning the population of Tel Aviv and Haifa.' Malware did not cause damage but represents an active intent to sabotage Israeli water OT.

incidents.vector: Unspecified cyberattack on April 17; surrounding Leon County disconnected its IT network to prevent contagion — language consistent with ransomware response.
incidents.impact: City IT network shut down; multiple city websites and public services remained unavailable for days.

incidents.victim: Global JavaScript developer ecosystem (incl. Microsoft Azure DevOps and OpenAI)
incidents.vector: Malicious versions of the widely-used Axios HTTP client (axios@1.14.1 and axios@0.30.4) briefly published to npm with a hidden dependency (plain-crypto-js@4.2.1) that executed at install time and reached out to attacker C2 sfrclak[.]com:8000 / 142.11.206.73 to retrieve a second-stage payload.
incidents.impact: Microsoft, Azure DevOps and OpenAI all issued guidance; OpenAI's Mac apps required patching. CrowdStrike attributed the campaign to STARDUST CHOLLIMA. Any CI/CD pipeline that resolved the malicious versions during the window must rotate credentials and treat artifacts as untrusted.
